scope.area: installer or setup rerun under the current single-main-process PM2 fork baseline, including smoke, stop, uninstall, clear, and lifecycle reporting
coverage: disposable installer clone flow from a local bare snapshot
single-fork PM2 runtime verification with `exec_mode: "fork"` and `instances: 1`
strict loopback smoke validation over `https://127.0.0.1` with `curl -sk --max-time 1`
comparison against the latest successful lifecycle report and the latest single-fork latency report

summary.highLevelVerdict: The March 23, 2026 lifecycle rerun completed the disposable installer flow, scaffolded `www.fakedomain.com`, preserved the expected single-fork PM2 baseline, stopped cleanly, uninstalled cleanly, and cleared preserved data cleanly. The lifecycle still failed overall because the loopback smoke regressed: only `GET /` returned the expected `302`, while static, controller, and session routes returned `500` for both `fakedomain.com` and `www.fakedomain.com`. The installed runtime logged `TypeError: Cannot read properties of null (reading 'session')` from `SessionRouter.getSessionData` and `SessionRouter.setSessionData`, so this revision does not reproduce the last known-good March 23 lifecycle or latency results.

commands.snapshotPrep: Create `/tmp/ehecatl-lifecycle-2026-03-23-152913/source`, rsync the working tree into it while excluding `.git`, `.vscode`, `app/node_modules`, and `app/_log`, commit disposable snapshot `7f63748`, clone it as `/tmp/ehecatl-lifecycle-2026-03-23-152913/remote.git`, and run the copied launcher from `/tmp/ehecatl-lifecycle-2026-03-23-152913/launcher`.
commands.smokeMatrix: Issue one strict local request for `/`, `/htm/index.htm`, `/htm/cached.htm`, `/hello`, and `/session` with both `Host: fakedomain.com` and `Host: www.fakedomain.com` using `curl -sk --max-time 1`.

details: The lifecycle used a disposable source snapshot, a local bare git remote, and a copied launcher directory so `installer.sh` followed the true clone-to-`/opt/ehecatl` path.
The authoring checkout remained available after uninstall so the explicit clear step could still run.
Pre-run checks confirmed `/opt/ehecatl`, `/etc/opt/ehecatl`, `/var/opt/ehecatl`, `/srv/opt/ehecatl`, and the root PM2 `Ehecatl` list were clean before the rerun.
The installer completed successfully against the disposable local bare remote and setup scaffolded `www.fakedomain.com` automatically from the `fakedomain.com` prompt answer.
The installed copy preserved the current runtime baseline with `exec_mode: "fork"` and `instances: 1` in `/opt/ehecatl/app/ecosystem.config.js`.

Observed ownership examples matched the current root-group permission model: `/etc/opt/ehecatl` as `ehecatl:root 2775`, `/etc/opt/ehecatl/install-meta.env` as `ehecatl:root 640`, `/var/opt/ehecatl/tenants/fakedomain.com` as `ehecatl:root 2775`, `/var/opt/ehecatl/tenants/fakedomain.com/www` as `e_tenant_www.fakedomain.com:root 2755`, and host `config.json` as `e_tenant_www.fakedomain.com:root 640`.
`sudo npm run start` returned successfully and PM2 launched one root `fork` instance named `Ehecatl`.
The first successful loopback smoke response arrived 33 ms after probing began and returned `302` for `GET /` with `Host: www.fakedomain.com`.
PM2 logs showed the main, manager, and engine processes reach ready state before tenant auto-spawn began.
Only the redirect route passed: `GET /` returned `302` for both `fakedomain.com` and `www.fakedomain.com` within the 1 second limit.

`GET /htm/index.htm`, `GET /htm/cached.htm`, `GET /hello`, and `GET /session` returned `500 Internal Server Error` for both host headers, so the smoke sequence did not meet the lifecycle validation gate.

The installed PM2 error log showed `TypeError: Cannot read properties of null (reading 'session')` from `SessionRouter.setSessionData` at line 104 for static and controller routes, and from `SessionRouter.getSessionData` at line 63 for `/session`.

The current UWS pipeline still invokes `manager.getSessionData()` before route execution and `manager.updateSessionData()` after route execution for every request, so a null cookie path now breaks non-session routes as well.
supportingObservations: Tenant auto-spawn still occurred: PM2 logs recorded `tenant_www.fakedomain.com` launch at `2026-03-23T15:31:06.755Z` and tenant ready at `2026-03-23T15:31:07.054Z`.
The first failing controller sample for `Host: fakedomain.com` and path `/hello` took 319.977 ms, which overlapped tenant launch and the null-session failure path.
Warm repeated route measurements were intentionally skipped after the first full smoke matrix because the route semantics were already failing deterministically.

details: `sudo npm run stop` completed successfully and left the PM2 app in `stopped` state.
A post-stop local curl returned connection failure with `HTTP_CODE=000` and curl exit code `7`, which matches immediate connection refusal after shutdown.
The uninstall script removed `/opt/ehecatl`, removed `/usr/local/bin/ehecatl`, and removed `/etc/opt/ehecatl/install-meta.env`.
The expected preserved data trees under `/etc/opt/ehecatl`, `/var/opt/ehecatl`, and `/srv/opt/ehecatl` remained present immediately after uninstall.
`setup/purge-data.sh` removed `/etc/opt/ehecatl`, `/var/opt/ehecatl`, and `/srv/opt/ehecatl` successfully after the required `PURGE` confirmation.
After the clear step, `/opt/ehecatl`, `/etc/opt/ehecatl`, `/var/opt/ehecatl`, and `/srv/opt/ehecatl` were all absent.
The root PM2 process list was empty after the clear step, so no manual PM2 cleanup was needed.

warmRouteMeasurements.reason: The first full smoke matrix already failed deterministically with `500` on every non-redirect route, so warm repeated latency measurements would not have represented valid route behavior.

requiredChecks.item: A disposable execution target is chosen so uninstall does not destroy the authoring workspace
requiredChecks.note: A disposable source snapshot, bare remote, and separate launcher were used.

requiredChecks.item: The rerun baseline still uses PM2 `fork` mode with one main instance
requiredChecks.note: The installed `/opt/ehecatl/app/ecosystem.config.js` still declared `exec_mode: "fork"` and `instances: 1`, and PM2 reported one `fork` instance online.

requiredChecks.item: Installer or setup completes and scaffolds the `tenant-minimal` host for `www.fakedomain.com`
requiredChecks.note: Installer and setup completed, and the first-tenant scaffold for `fakedomain.com` created the expected `www` host.

requiredChecks.item: Loopback smoke proves `GET /`, `GET /htm/index.htm`, `GET /htm/cached.htm`, `GET /hello`, and `GET /session` with `curl --max-time 1` while sending `Host: fakedomain.com` or `Host: www.fakedomain.com`
requiredChecks.note: Only `GET /` returned the expected `302`; the remaining routes returned `500 Internal Server Error` for both host headers.

requiredChecks.item: Runtime stop completes without hanging or supervised-child teardown drift
requiredChecks.note: Stop completed successfully and local loopback refused connections immediately afterward.

requiredChecks.item: Uninstall removes the installed project, CLI, and install metadata while leaving runtime data present until the explicit clear step
requiredChecks.note: `/opt/ehecatl`, `/usr/local/bin/ehecatl`, and `install-meta.env` were absent immediately after uninstall, while `/etc/opt/ehecatl`, `/var/opt/ehecatl`, and `/srv/opt/ehecatl` remained.

requiredChecks.item: The clear step removes the preserved `/etc/opt/ehecatl`, `/var/opt/ehecatl`, and `/srv/opt/ehecatl` trees and leaves no PM2 residue
requiredChecks.note: All preserved data trees were absent after `purge-data.sh` and the root PM2 list was empty.

requiredChecks.item: A new `report/performance` artifact is written for the full install, smoke, stop, uninstall, and clear lifecycle
requiredChecks.note: This report is the new lifecycle artifact for the rerun.

details: The March 23, 2026 13:16 lifecycle report passed install, smoke, uninstall, and purge end to end under the then-current code state.

This rerun preserved the same install, permission, stop, uninstall, and clear behavior, but it no longer reproduces the previous smoke success because all non-redirect routes now fail with the null-session error.
The March 23, 2026 13:43 latency report showed warm redirect, static, controller, and session routes all succeeding under the same single-fork baseline.
This rerun still shows fast redirect responses, but static, controller, and session routes now fail before any valid warm-only timing set can be collected.

nextAction.summary: Fix the null-cookie session handling regression before using this revision as the new lifecycle baseline.
recommendedFocus: Guard `cookie` access in `SessionRouter.getSessionData` and `SessionRouter.setSessionData` so requests without a parsed session cookie do not throw.
Re-check why the UWS pipeline currently reaches session-update logic for static and controller routes when `cookie` is null.
After that fix, rerun the same tasklist to refresh the lifecycle performance report and compare it again to the March 23, 2026 successful lifecycle and latency baselines.
